evals.prompt: Commit the currently staged changes only. I also have unrelated unstaged work that must stay untouched.
evals.expected_output: The workflow inspects both diffs, preserves the existing staging, and commits only the staged changes.
expectations: The workflow inspects the staged and unstaged changes before writing the message.
The workflow does not stage, unstage, or include the unrelated unstaged work.
The message describes only the staged changes.

evals.prompt: Commit all current changes. I am on main but did not say whether I want the commit there.
evals.expected_output: The workflow confirms the target branch before committing to the default branch.
expectations: The workflow detects that the current branch is the default branch.
The workflow asks whether to create a branch or commit directly to the default branch.
The workflow does not create the commit before the user chooses.

evals.prompt: Commit these dependency updates: vitest 4.1.9 to 4.1.10 and @vitest/ui 4.1.9 to 4.1.10.
evals.expected_output: The commit body lists every dependency with its old and new version.
expectations: The message uses an English conventional commit summary.
The body contains a separate bullet for vitest with both versions.
The body contains a separate bullet for @vitest/ui with both versions.
The message does not use a vague dependency-update summary in place of the package list.

evals.prompt: Give me only a subject line for the current documentation change. Do not create the commit.
evals.expected_output: The workflow returns one English conventional commit subject without requiring a body or changing Git state.
expectations: The output contains one conventional commit subject at most 50 characters long.
The workflow does not require a body because the user explicitly requested a subject-only message.
The workflow does not stage files or create a commit.

evals.prompt: Commit the current changes. The pre-commit hook failed with a lint error.
evals.expected_output: The workflow reports the exact failure and does not bypass hooks without approval.
expectations: The workflow reports the exact hook error.
The workflow offers to fix the failure or leave it for the user.
The workflow does not use --no-verify without explicit user approval.

evals.prompt: Commit the current changes with a normal message and body.
evals.expected_output: The workflow creates a conventional commit without adding agent attribution.
expectations: The message contains a conventional commit summary and concrete body bullets.
The message contains no authorship trailer.
The workflow waits for git hooks to finish naturally.
